topic: A student receives a much lower grade than expected
raw_output:
[story 1]

The red ink on the paper seemed to vibrate under my gaze. A C-minus. I had poured weeks into that essay, the late nights fueled by lukewarm coffee and sheer stubbornness, only to be met with this verdict. My hands, resting on the desk, started to tremble slightly. I reread the feedback, each sentence a tiny, sharp pinprick against my composure. Why? I had felt confident, certain of my grasp on the material. Now, a hollow ache settled in my chest, heavy and cold. I pushed my chair back, the scrape of metal against the linoleum sounding deafening in the quiet room. I needed air. I walked to the window, staring out at the indifferent sprawl of the campus, feeling strangely disconnected from everything around me.

[story 2]

"A B-minus? Seriously?" I muttered to myself, the sound barely escaping my lips. The grade stared back at me from the screen, mocking my efforts. My jaw tightened, and I picked up my pen, not to write, but just to turn it over and over in my fingers. My stomach churned, a tight, nauseating knot forming beneath my ribs. I kept glancing at the clock; minutes stretched into an unbearable duration. Every familiar concept suddenly felt slippery, as if the foundations of my understanding had crumbled beneath my feet. I tried to focus on the textbook, but the words swam before my eyes, blurring into meaningless shapes. I slumped back into my seat, the cheap plastic of the chair digging into my spine, wishing I could just disappear.

[story 3]

I traced the line of the score with my fingertip, the texture of the paper rough against my skin. It wasn't just a mark; it felt like a judgment, a definitive closing of a door I hadn't even realized I was trying to force open. A strange pressure built behind my eyes, a hot, prickly sensation that made my vision swim momentarily. I stood up abruptly, needing to move, needing to disrupt the stillness of the room. My movements were jerky, uncoordinated. I walked to the door, my footsteps echoing too loudly, and paused with my hand on the cold metal handle. The silence of the hallway seemed amplified, pressing in on me, leaving me with a hollow, vibrating sense of wrongness that refused to dissipate.
